title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
abstract: The paper proposes an approach called Reflexion that uses verbal reinforcement to help agents learn from prior failings. Reflexion converts binary or scalar feedback from the environment into verbal feedback in the form of a textual summary, which is then added as additional context for the LLM agent in the next episode.

methodology.description: Uses verbal reinforcement to help agents learn from prior failings by converting binary or scalar feedback from the environment into verbal feedback in the form of a textual summary
feedback_generation_methods: Pre-defined heuristics for common failure cases
Self-evaluation using LLMs (for decision-making) or self-written unit tests (for programming)
methodology.memory_mechanism: Evaluation signal is converted to natural language experience summaries which can be stored in long-term memory
methodology.comparison_to_human_learning: Similar to how humans iteratively learn to accomplish complex tasks in a few-shot manner by reflecting on their previous failures

advantages: Lightweight and doesn't require finetuning the LLM
Allows for more nuanced forms of feedback
Allows for explicit and interpretable episodic memory
Provides explicit hints for actions in future episodes
disadvantages: Relies on the power of the LLM's self-evaluation capabilities

task_types: Decision-making tasks (sequential action choices over long trajectories)
Programming tasks (teaching the agent to use external tools)

results.programming: Improvement on Python programming tasks on HumanEval

comparison_to_traditional_rl: Compared to traditional RL approaches like policy or value-based learning
